_comment: Global market assumptions shared across ALL profiles. No withdrawal sequences here — those are per-profile in economic.json.

overrides._comment: Years 1-5: conservative rebalancing, standard cash reserve
Years 6-15: slightly relaxed capgain limit as brokerage grows
Years 16-30: reduced cash reserve months, higher capgain limit

readme.purpose: Global market behavior settings shared across ALL profiles. Overrides section applies year-range-specific changes. Profile economic.json is merged on top for profile-specific differences — profile values win on any conflict.

bad_market.drawdown_threshold: Portfolio decline fraction from peak that triggers bad-market mode. E.g. 0.15 = 15% below peak switches to bad-market withdrawal sequencing and suppresses rebalancing.
bad_market.use_recession_flag: true = also trigger bad-market mode if the stochastic model sets a macro recession flag, even if drawdown threshold has not yet been crossed.
bad_market.p10_signal_enabled: true = also trigger bad-market mode when the cross-sectional P10 annual return across all paths is below p10_return_threshold_pct. This is a leading indicator — catches deteriorating market conditions before any single path hits the drawdown threshold.
bad_market.p10_return_threshold_pct: P10 return level (%) below which bad-market mode triggers. E.g. -2.0 = if the 10th-percentile path is returning worse than -2% this year, treat as bad market even if the median path is still positive.

cash_reserve.months: Months of withdrawal target kept as liquid cash buffer. E.g. 6 = six-month cash cushion always maintained in brokerage.
cash_reserve.allow_dip_below_reserve: false = never let cash fall below the reserve floor even if it means a withdrawal shortfall.
cash_reserve.use_cash_only_in_bad_markets: true = only draw from the cash reserve during bad markets; use investment accounts in good markets.

rebalancing.global_drift_threshold: Maximum allowed drift as a fraction of target weight before rebalancing triggers. E.g. 0.12 = ±12% of target weight. Overrides the per-portfolio rebalance_band_pct default from allocation_yearly.json when set here.
rebalancing.require_cash_above_reserve_to_rebalance: true = do not execute rebalancing trades if cash is at or below the reserve threshold.
rebalancing.suppress_in_bad_market: true = pause all rebalancing during bad-market periods to avoid locking in losses at depressed prices.
rebalancing.brokerage_capgain_limit_k: Maximum capital gains in $k per year realized from brokerage rebalancing. Tax note: brokerage rebalancing generates taxable capital gains — this cap prevents a single rebalance from creating a large unexpected tax bill. TRAD and Roth rebalancing generates no taxable event.
rebalancing.brokerage_enabled: true/false. Master switch for brokerage rebalancing. TRAD and Roth accounts always rebalance without tax consequence.

withdrawals.shock_scaling_enabled: true = scale down take-home withdrawals during bad markets. false = withdraw full target regardless. Note: even when scaled down, taxes are still computed on actual income (RMDs, conversions) and debited from brokerage separately.
withdrawals.drawdown_threshold: Portfolio decline fraction at which scaling begins. Should match bad_market.drawdown_threshold for consistent behavior.
withdrawals.min_scaling_factor: Minimum fraction of the target withdrawal paid out. E.g. 0.65 = never deliver less than 65% of planned take-home. Works together with floor_k in withdrawal_schedule.json — the effective floor is max(floor_k, min_scaling_factor × target).

scale_curve_options.linear: Take-home scales linearly from 100% at threshold down to min_scaling_factor at full drawdown.
scale_curve_options.poly: Polynomial scaling. scale_poly_alpha > 1 = gentle reduction at first, sharper near the bottom.
scale_curve_options.exp: Exponential scaling. scale_exp_lambda controls how quickly reduction accelerates.

withdrawals.use_recession_flag: Same as bad_market — also scale withdrawals when recession flag is set.
withdrawals.makeup_enabled: true = in good years following a scaled-down year, make up a portion of the missed take-home.
withdrawals.makeup_ratio: Fraction of the cumulative missed withdrawal recovered per good year. E.g. 0.3 = recover 30% of the deficit each year.
withdrawals.makeup_cap_per_year: Maximum makeup payment as fraction of that year's target. E.g. 0.1 = makeup never exceeds 10% of the year's planned withdrawal.
withdrawals.upside_scaling_enabled: false = withdrawals never exceed amount_k (default). true = when portfolio outperforms baseline median path by outperformance_threshold, scale withdrawals up toward upside_max_factor × amount_k. Only applies in automatic and retirement modes.
withdrawals.upside_outperformance_threshold: Portfolio outperformance fraction above which upside scaling begins. E.g. 0.15 = portfolio must be >15% above expected median before withdrawals are raised.
withdrawals.upside_max_factor: Maximum upside withdrawal as a multiple of amount_k. E.g. 1.25 = cap at 125% of planned withdrawal in the best years.
withdrawals.upside_scale_curve: Shape of upside scaling: linear (default), poly, or exp. Same semantics as scale_curve for downside.

readme.overrides_note: Time-bounded only. Each override applies for its years range then reverts to the defaults block. To make a permanent change, update the defaults block instead of adding a permanent override.
readme.core_inflation_pct: Long-run core inflation assumption (%). Used as the fallback rate when inflation_yearly.json does not cover a simulation year. Fed long-run target is ~2.0%; recent 2022-2024 experience was 4-9%; 2.5% is a reasonable baseline. Override in inflation_yearly.json for specific year ranges.
